sentence_reviews.text: Predictive distributions and belief states express uncertainty rather than only point estimates.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML probabilistic prediction material; AIMA probabilistic reasoning and belief-state material.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited sources support distributions and belief states as representations of uncertainty. The contrast with point estimates is a concise synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Probability theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML and AIMA probabilistic reasoning chapters treat distributions and beliefs as probability-based constructs.
sentence_reviews.notes: Probability theory directly underlies distributions and belief-state uncertainty representations.

sentence_reviews.text: Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Statistics & experimental design.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML statistical learning and probabilistic prediction material.
sentence_reviews.notes: Statistical estimation and predictive uncertainty support this dependency, though experimental design is not directly shown by the packet source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs is modeled as depending on Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design and feeding downstream nodes such as Contrastive learning, Generative modeling, Calibration, uncertainty & risk objectives.

sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.

edge_reviews.notes: Distributions and belief states are probability-theoretic objects, so requires/core is directly supported.
Statistics is central to estimating and evaluating predictive distributions, but the edge is less exact than the probability dependency.
